channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Name That Toon Contest
New SilabRAT Trojan Hijacks Sessions to Steal Crypto
item.description: MaaS trojan SilabRAT uses HVNC and browser cloning to hijack sessions and steal crypto

item.title: Cybersecurity Software Fails to Detect Fifth of Brower-Based Phishing Attacks
item.description: Menlo Security research warns that as enterprise applications become increasingly browser based, traditional cybersecurity tools leave them vulnerable to cyber threats

item.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, and SAP Release Patches for Multiple Critical Vulnerabilities
item.description:
Fortinet, Ivanti, and SAP have released security updates to address multiple critical security vulnerabilities that could result in arbitrary code execution and information disclosure.
The security flaw patched by Fortinet relates to a command injection vulnerability in FortiSandbox, FortiSandbox Cloud, and FortiSandbox PaaS WEB UI. It's tracked as CVE-2026-25089 (CVSS score: 9.1).
"An
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Unpatched Langflow Flaw CVE-2026-5027 Exploited for Unauthenticated RCE
item.description:
A high-severity unpatched security flaw in Langflow, an open-source low-code platform to build artificial intelligence (AI) applications, has come under active exploitation in the wild, according to findings from VulnCheck.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-5027 (CVSS score: 8.8), a case of path traversal that could allow an attacker to write files to arbitrary locations.
"The 'POST /
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Adds Cisco, Chrome, and Arista Flaws to KEV Catalog Amid Active Exploitation
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added three new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, following reports of active exploitation.
The list of vulnerabilities is as follows -
CVE-2026-20245 (CVSS score: 7.8) - An improper encoding or escaping of output vulnerability in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager that could allow an
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
item.description: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: Infostealers Turn Millions of Devices Into Credential Theft Machines
item.description: <p>As attackers increasingly favor stolen credentials over exploits, infostealers have become a primary source of access for ransomware and other cybercrime operations.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/infostealers-turn-millions-of-devices-into-credential-theft-machines/">Infostealers Turn Millions of Devices Into Credential Theft Machines</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kevin Townsend

item.title: Cyera Raises $600 Million at $12 Billion Valuation
item.description: <p>Cyera is positioned as one of the most valuable privately held cybersecurity firms in the world with total funding topping $2 billion.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/cyera-raises-600-million-at-12-billion-valuation/">Cyera Raises $600 Million at $12 Billion Valuation</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Microsoft’s Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday Fixes Over 200 Security Flaws
item.description: <p>Microsoft’s June Patch Tuesday fixed about 200 CVEs, including public zero-days and Critical Windows flaws tied to patching pressure.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-june-patch-tuesday-record-breaking/">Microsoft’s Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday Fixes Over 200 Security Flaws</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Aryon Security Raises $29 Million in Series A Funding
item.description: <p>In the post-Mythos era, the company’s platform helps organizations enforce security controls across environments.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/aryon-security-raises-29-million-in-series-a-funding/">Aryon Security Raises $29 Million in Series A Funding</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Critical HVAC and UPS Vulnerabilities Could Let Hackers Disrupt Data Centers
item.description: <p>Claroty researchers have analyzed the security of Vertiv UPS network cards and the Trane Tracer SC+ HVAC controller.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/critical-hvac-and-ups-vulnerabilities-could-let-hackers-disrupt-data-centers/">Critical HVAC and UPS Vulnerabilities Could Let Hackers Disrupt Data Centers</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: CISO Forum Webinar Today: 2026 Mid-Year Review
item.description: <p>Learn more about protecting against unmonitored use of generative AI (Shadow AI) in business units and building and enforcing AI governance frameworks.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/ciso-forum-webinar-today-2026-mid-year-review/">CISO Forum Webinar Today: 2026 Mid-Year Review</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek News

item.title: New Windows Zero-Day Exploit ‘RoguePlanet’ Released
item.description: <p>Exploiting a race condition in Microsoft Defender, the exploit leads to local privilege escalation to SYSTEM.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/new-windows-zero-day-exploit-rogueplanet-released/">New Windows Zero-Day Exploit &#8216;RoguePlanet&#8217; Released</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: NSO Group Hacking WhatsApp Despite Court Order
item.description: <p>WhatsApp has <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/whatsapp-catches-spyware-firm-nso-defying-no-hacking-court-order/">caught</a> the NSO Group phishing its users, in violation of a court order.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: New Fable 5 Is a "Mythos-Class" LLM Available to All, Anthropic Announces
item.description: Anthropic unveils Claude Mythos 5 and Fable 5, a restricted-access frontier AI model and guardrailed version for everyone to use

item.title: After AI Reaches Production: 12 Ways Security Teams Can Take Control
item.description: <p>Security teams need more than visibility into AI applications, they need a repeatable framework for monitoring, investigating, and defending them in production.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/after-ai-reaches-production-12-ways-security-teams-can-take-control/">After AI Reaches Production: 12 Ways Security Teams Can Take Control</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joshua Goldfarb

item.title: Your Automated Pentest Looks Clean. See What It Missed in This Expert Webinar
item.description:
Your pentest report looks clean. That might be the problem.
Run automated pentesting long enough, and the new findings start to dry up. By the third or fourth run, fewer issues appear. The report looks stable. Leadership reads "stable" as "secure." It usually isn't. The work slows down. The risk does not.
That gap is what a The Hacker News webinar with Picus Security sets out to close.
Autumn
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Over a Quarter of Identity Crime Victims Hit by Multiple Incidents, ITRC Data Shows
item.description: Nearly 26% of identity crime victims faced multiple incidents in the past year, as ITRC warns of a growing "multi-layered crisis"

item.title: ServiceNow Patches Vulnerability Exploited Against Some Customers
item.description: <p>The company updated hosted customer instances to patch a security issue it reportedly had known about since April 7.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/servicenow-patches-vulnerability-exploited-against-some-customers/">ServiceNow Patches Vulnerability Exploited Against Some Customers</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Microsoft Patches Record 206 Flaws, Including Three Zero-Days and Critical RCE Bugs
item.description:
Microsoft on Tuesday released fixes for a record 206 security vulnerabilities impacting its software portfolio, including three flaws that have been publicly disclosed at the time of release.
Of the 206 flaws, 39 are rated Critical, and 167 are rated Important in severity. This includes 63 privilege escalation, 56 remote code execution, 30 information disclosure, 27 spoofing, 20 security
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Critical Vulnerabilities Patched in Fortinet, Ivanti Products
item.description: <p>Two OS command injection flaws can be exploited remotely, without authentication, for arbitrary code execution.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/critical-vulnerabilities-patched-in-fortinet-ivanti-products/">Critical Vulnerabilities Patched in Fortinet, Ivanti Products</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Microsoft Fixes 200 CVEs in June Patch Tuesday
item.description: Microsoft has patched 200 vulnerabilities including three zero-days

item.title: ICS Patch Tuesday: Vulnerabilities Fixed by Siemens, Schneider, Phoenix Contact
item.description: <p>In addition, Rockwell Automation announced some enhancements to its SecureOT cybersecurity solution for OT.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/ics-patch-tuesday-vulnerabilities-fixed-by-siemens-schneider-phoenix-contact/">ICS Patch Tuesday: Vulnerabilities Fixed by Siemens, Schneider, Phoenix Contact</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Fable 5, Its Most Powerful AI Yet, With Cyber Safeguards
item.description:
On June 9, Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, the most capable model it has ever made, generally available. It also did something unusual: it shipped one model as two products, split not by capability but by a layer of safety classifiers.
Fable 5 goes to the public. Its twin, Claude Mythos 5, the same underlying model with the cyber safeguards lifted, stays locked to a vetted group of cyber
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ServiceNow Flaw Exploited to Gain Unauthorized Access to Customer Instances
item.description:
ServiceNow has warned about a security incident in which unknown threat actors exploited a flaw to obtain deeper unauthorized access to susceptible instances.
"On June 5, 2026, ServiceNow applied a security update to hosted customer instances," the company revealed in an advisory that requires customer access. "The update concerned a security issue that could allow an unauthenticated user, in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Defender RoguePlanet Zero-Day Grants SYSTEM Access on Updated Windows
item.description:
The anonymous security researcher going by the name Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse) has released a proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit for yet another Microsoft Defender zero-day named RoguePlanet.
"The exploit is a race condition, so it's a hit or miss," the researcher, who published the exploit under a new GitHub account, "MSNightmare" said. "I have managed to get a 100% success rate on
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Six Proto6 Vulnerabilities in protobuf.js Expose Node.js Apps to RCE and DoS
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged half a dozen vulnerabilities in protobuf.js, a JavaScript and TypeScript implementation of Protocol Buffers (Protobuf), that, if successfully exploited, could result in remote code execution (RCE) and denial-of-service (DoS) attacks.
"In affected environments, a single malicious protobuf schema, descriptor, or crafted payload could be enough to trigger
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
item.description: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and exploit code for at least three of the weaknesses is now publicly available.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: The Invisible Battlefield: How Cyber War Is Reshaping Everyday Life
item.description: Former National Cyber Director Chris Inglis warns that cyber attacks threaten hospitals, utilities and essential services.
item.dc:creator: Chris Inglis

item.title: Blame AI: Patch Tuesday Hits Record 206 CVEs
item.description: Voluminous patch updates could soon be the norm, as artificial intelligence accelerates the speed and scale of vulnerability discovery.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Microsoft Exchange Flaw Lets Attackers Spoof Any Email Address
item.description: “Ghost-Sender&quot; is the result of a widespread misconfiguration, according to researchers, and evidence indicates it's being actively abused in the wild.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Miasma Supply Chain Worm Burrows Into 73 Microsoft Repositories
item.description: The attacks stemmed from a GitHub account that was also compromised in a previous Miasmi attack on Microsoft last month.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Check Point Warning: Actively Exploited VPN Zero-Day Linked to Qilin Ransomware
item.description: <p>Check Point says VPN zero-day CVE-2026-50751 was exploited by a Qilin-linked actor, prompting emergency hotfixes and a CISA patch deadline.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-checkpoint-vpn-zero-day-ransomware/">Check Point Warning: Actively Exploited VPN Zero-Day Linked to Qilin Ransomware</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Meta to Use Off-Site Business Data for Feed and AI Personalization
item.description:
Meta on Tuesday announced that it will use information shared by other businesses to personalize users' feed and responses from its artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot, expanding its scope beyond targeted ads.
"Businesses often share information about people's activity on their sites with us to make ads more relevant," Meta said in a statement.
"We already use this data - like games you play
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Veeam Backup & Replication RCE Flaw Lets Domain Users Run Remote Code
item.description:
Veeam has released security patches to address a critical flaw in its Backup &amp; Replication software that could result in remote code execution.
Tracked as CVE-2026-44963, the vulnerability carries a CVSS score of 9.4 out of a maximum of 10.0.
"A vulnerability allowing remote code execution (RCE) on the Backup Server by an authenticated domain user," Veeam said in a Tuesday advisory.
It
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Restores Some GitHub Repos, Keeps Others Offline as Miasma Probe Continues
item.description:
Microsoft on Monday confirmed that it temporarily removed some GitHub repositories in response to a recent security incident that led to 73 of its open-source projects being compromised to inject an information stealer into the code.
"Our priority is to protect customers and the broader ecosystem," a Microsoft spokesperson told The Hacker News via email. "We temporarily removed some
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Russian Attackers Weaponize WinRAR Flaw Against Ukrainian Orgs
item.description: Two separate campaigns target CVE-2025-8088, fixed last July, to conduct data theft and cyberespionage against military and government targets in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: 75% of Firms Deploy Vulnerable Code Amid Pressure on CISOs, Report Finds
item.description: Checkmarx report warns that business pressure is among the reason security leaders let security compliance slip

item.title: GPS As a Key Distribution Platform
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: AI Coding Adoption Hits 97% but Governance Lags Behind
item.description: Most dev teams use AI coding assistants but only 30% have full governance in place

item.title: Critical phpBB Flaw Lets Attackers Hijack Any Account with One Request
item.description: Critical phpBB authentication bypass lets attackers hijack any account with one request

item.title: Meta Instagram Recovery Flaw Exposed More Than 20,000 Accounts
item.description: <p>Meta says a bug in its AI-assisted account recovery workflow likely let attackers reset passwords for more than 20,000 Instagram accounts.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-meta-instagram-recovery-flaw-20k/">Meta Instagram Recovery Flaw Exposed More Than 20,000 Accounts</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: WinRAR Flaw Exploited by Russia-Aligned Groups to Deploy Stealers in Ukraine
item.description:
Two Russia-aligned cyber attack campaigns have continued to exploit a security flaw in WinRAR to target Ukrainian organisations, almost a year after patches for the vulnerability were released.
The activity has been attributed by Trend Micro to Earth Dahu (aka Gamaredon) and SHADOW-EARTH-066 (aka UAC-0226). It involves the exploitation of CVE-2025-8088, a path traversal flaw that allows an
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Researchers Build Self-Replicating AI Worm That Operates Entirely on Local, Open-Weight Models
item.description:
University of Toronto researchers have built and tested a proof-of-concept AI-driven computer worm that uses a locally hosted open-weight large language model to reason its way through a network, generate tailored attack strategies for each target it encounters, and replicate itself, all without human intervention and without touching a commercial AI service.
The preprint, posted to arXiv on
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Chrome V8 Zero-Day CVE-2026-11645 Exploited in the Wild - Patch Now
item.description:
Google has released security updates to address 74 vulnerabilities, including one that has come under active exploitation in the wild.
The high-severity vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-11645 (CVSS score: 8.8), has been described as an out-of-bounds memory access in V8, Chrome's JavaScript and WebAssembly engine.
"Out-of-bounds read and write in V8 in Google Chrome prior to 149.0.7827.103
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Hidden Security Risk in Modern Networks: The Work Between Tools
item.description:
Organizations have more visibility than ever. Growing tech stacks provide greater coverage, and network security teams are increasingly adopting AI and automation to help with routine tasks and reduce manual effort.
But the same challenges persist. Outages still last hours, causing significant financial losses, operational disruption, and reputational impact. Threat response and mean time to
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Releases Patch for Chrome Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
item.description: The flaw, CVE-2026-11645, can allow a remote attacker to execute arbitrary code inside a sandbox via a crafted HTML page

item.title: New FROST Attack Lets Websites Track What Sites and Apps You Open via SSD Timing
item.description:
A malicious website can work out which sites you visit and which apps you open, using nothing but JavaScript and the timing of your SSD. The attack, called FROST, needs no native code, no extension, and no permission prompt.
You open the page, leave the tab sitting there, and it watches the drive for contention in the background.
Researchers at Graz University of Technology built it and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Check Point Warns Critical Auth Bypass Bug Exploited in the Wild
item.description: Check Point says a critical vulnerability in its Remote Access VPN and Mobile Access solutions has been exploited by Qilin

item.title: Hades PyPI Attack: 19 Packages Poisoned to Auto-Run Bun Credential Stealer
item.description:
The Miasma supply chain campaign has sparked a fresh attack wave called Hades, this time involving 37 malicious wheel artifacts across 19 packages in the Python Package Index (PyPI) registry, as the Mini Shai-Hulud-style attacks continue to be refined and splintered to target specific ecosystems.
"The compromised releases shipped a *-setup.pth file that attempts to execute automatically
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: Why JLR’s CISO Enforced In-Person Password Resets Following Cyber-Attack
item.description: Speaking at Infosecurity Europe, Ashish Shrestha, former CISO at Jaguar Land Rover revealed why he wanted over 30,000 employees to change their passwords in the immediate aftermath of the incident

item.title: Cybercriminals: the 'auditors' you never hired
item.description: Every organisation gets audited. The question is who does the auditing.

item.title: WhatsApp Discovers NSO Group-Linked Spearphishing Attempts
item.description: Meta’s WhatsApp demands contempt ruling after users report NSO Group-linked phishing

item.title: LiteLLM Flaw CVE-2026-42271 Exploited in the Wild, Chains to Unauthenticated RCE
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added a high-severity flaw impacting BerriAI LiteLLM to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-42271 (CVSS score: 8.7), is a command injection vulnerability that could allow any authenticated user to run arbitrary commands on the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Slop Will Kill Cybersecurity Storytelling If We Let It
item.description: AI-generated content threatens credibility in cybersecurity. This &quot;Ask the Expert&quot; column explores why human oversight matters and how to maintain authentic narratives.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Safran

item.title: Silent Ransom Group Hits US Law Firms in Escalating Extortion Attacks
item.description: The financially motivated group is combining vishing, IT impersonation, and in-person office intrusions to steal data and extort victims.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Check Point VPN Flaw Exploited Since Early May
item.description: A newly discovered, critical zero-day vulnerability is under attack; a Qilin ransomware affiliate has been blamed for at least one incident.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: One-Character Linux Kernel Flaw Enables Local Root Access, Exploits Now Public
item.description:
Security researchers have published a detailed, working exploit for a Linux kernel use-after-free that lets an unprivileged local user escalate to root and break out of a container.
The flaw, CVE-2026-23111, sits in the kernel's nf_tables packet-filtering code and was patched upstream on February 5, 2026. Exodus Intelligence released its full technical walkthrough on June 8, and it is not even
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Iran Signed a Ceasefire — Its Hackers Didn't
item.description: An extension of the Geneva Conventions could impose restrictions on cyberwarfare under ceasefire conditions and close a major loophole in international conflict.
item.dc:creator: Emil Sayegh

item.title: Meta: NSO Tried Targeting WhatsApp Users Despite Court Order
item.description: <p>Meta says WhatsApp disrupted new NSO-linked phishing attacks and is asking a court to hold the spyware firm in contempt.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-whatsapp-nso-targeted-users-after-court-ban/">Meta: NSO Tried Targeting WhatsApp Users Despite Court Order</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Meta Blocks NSO Group's New WhatsApp Phishing Attack, Files Contempt Order
item.description:
Meta on Monday said it detected and blocked spear-phishing attempts linked to Israeli spyware vendor NSO Group.
In addition, the tech giant said it's filing a federal court contempt order against the company for violating a permanent injunction that barred it from targeting WhatsApp and its users.
"They tried to trick people into clicking on malicious links to drive them to external websites
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Critical Zcash Vulnerability Found and Fixed
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: 'Hades' Campaign Against PyPI Puts New Spin on Shai-Hulud
item.description: The latest attacks, which hit 37 PyPI wheels and 19 code packages, show a continued evolution of the persistent software supply chain threat.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: OpenAI Expands ChatGPT Lockdown Mode to Millions of Eligible Users
item.description: <p>OpenAI is expanding ChatGPT Lockdown Mode to more users, limiting web-connected tools to reduce the risks of prompt injection and data leakage.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-openai-expands-chatgpt-lockdown-mode-millions-users/">OpenAI Expands ChatGPT Lockdown Mode to Millions of Eligible Users</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: North Korean Hackers Use Fake Coding Tasks to Steal Crypto
item.description: North Korean actor UNK_DeadDrop targeted developers with fake coding tasks to steal crypto

item.title: Critical Check Point VPN Flaw Exploited to Bypass Passwords in IKEv1 Setups
item.description:
Check Point has warned of active exploitation of a critical vulnerability impacting Remote Access VPN and Mobile Access deployments that are configured to use the deprecated IKEv1 key exchange protocol.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-50751 (CVSS score: 9.3), is a case of a logic flow weakness in certificate validation that allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to bypass user
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: OpenAI Unveils ChatGPT Account Security Controls
item.description: OpenAI brings Lockdown Mode and Active Sessions to ChatGPT to curb prompt injection data theft

item.title: AI Phishing Is Crushing SOCs with Alert Volume: How to Reduce Tier 1 Overload
item.description:
Phishing has always been a numbers game. AI has turned it into a volume machine.
Attackers can now create convincing emails, fake login pages, and tailored lures in minutes. Every polished message adds another case for Tier 1 to review, another link to inspect, and another alert that cannot be dismissed at a glance.
As the queue grows, a credential theft attempt or malware delivery can easily
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Instagram Account Hacks, Android Zero-Day, GitHub Worm and More
item.description:
Monday again. The weekend was meant to be quiet. It wasn't. Last week had poisoned packages, a broken AI helper, and a worm tearing through repos. The ugly part: basic tricks still worked.
A chatbot got fooled. A bot token got leaked inside the malware. The same old mistakes showed up again. And while everyone chased the loud stuff, quieter attackers sat in inboxes for months, reading mail and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos AI Reportedly Enters NSA Offensive Cyber Planning
item.description: <p>Anthropic engineers are reportedly helping the NSA use Claude Mythos for cyber operations despite the Pentagon’s supply-chain risk label.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-anthropic-nsa-mythos-ai-cyber-operations/">Anthropic’s Mythos AI Reportedly Enters NSA Offensive Cyber Planning</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: David Curry

item.title: Spotlight On: Dreamplug Technologies Private Limited (CRED), a New Principal Participating Organization
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: The Hardest Fork
item.description: Mythos is real. I know a big chunk of the industry thinks it's a marketing stunt, and I get why. I get it. But I've seen the findings, and they're bad. These aren't "whoops, this line right here is wrong, and that's RCE." They're novel combinations of a few dozen issues out of thousands of things every SAST scanner already finds, chained together into something much worse. It's real creativity,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Anthropic’s Project Glasswing Update
item.description: <p>In April, Anthropic initated <a href="https://www.anthropic.com/glasswing">Project Glasswing</a>. The idea was to let companies use their new model to find and fix vulnerabilities in their own software. It was a fantastic PR move, and so many press outlets have uncritically parroted Anthropic&#8217;s claims that it&#8217;s now common wisdom that Mythos is better at finding software vulnerabilities than other models. Which is just <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/commentisfree/2026/may/08/how-dangerous-is-anthropics-mythos-ai">not</a> <a href="https://spectrum.ieee.org/ai-cybersecurity-mythos">true</a>.</p> <p>In any case, Anthropic has <a href="https://www.anthropic.com/research/glasswing-initial-update">published</a> a Project Glasswing status report. It&#8217;s finding <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/anthropic-mythos-detected-23000-potential-vulnerabilities-across-1000-oss-projects/">a lot</a> of vulnerabilities in software&#8212;yay! Some of them are even dangerous. But almost none of them has been patched. It&#8217;s ...</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: Prompt Injection Remains Unsolved, OWASP Researcher Warns
item.description: At Infosecurity Europe 2026, OWASP’s Ariel Fogel warned that prompt injection remains an “unresolved problem” within generative AI architecture

item.title: VerdantBamboo Deploys BSD Variant of BRICKSTORM on Linux Appliances
item.description:
A China-nexus cyber espionage group has been observed deploying a BSD variant of a known backdoor called BRICKSTORM, as well as two other malware families codenamed PLENET (aka GRIMBOLT) and AGENTPSD to target Linux systems.
The activity has been attributed by Volexity to a threat cluster it tracks as VerdantBamboo, which it said overlaps with hacking groups known as Clay Typhoon (Microsoft),
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Two-Thirds of Open Source Community Unaware of Cyber Resilience Act
Infosecurity Europe: How DSIT Protects Thousands of UK Orgs from Cyber Vulnerabilities
item.description: The Department of Science, Innovation and Technology details how a combination of hands-on human advice and technology systems keeps government agencies safe

item.title: Meta AI Bug Exposes Over 20,000 Instagram Accounts
item.description: Meta confirms an AI tool vulnerability led to unauthorized access to Instagram accounts after a failure in email verification during password reset

item.title: UNC3753 Used Vishing and Physical Intrusions in U.S. Data Theft Extortion Campaign
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a financially motivated data theft extortion campaign that has targeted dozens of organizations across professional, legal, and financial services in the U.S. between January and May 2026.
The activity has been attributed by Google Mandiant and Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) to a threat actor dubbed UNC3753, which is also known as
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: VS Code Adds 2-Hour Extension Auto-Update Delay to Limit Supply Chain Attacks
item.description:
Microsoft has announced that Visual Studio Code (VS Code) will apply a two-hour delay before extensions for the integrated development environment (IDE) are updated automatically to a newer version in an attempt to tackle software supply chain threats.
"When automatic updates are enabled, new versions are auto-updated two hours after they are published, adding an extra layer of protection
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New ChatGPT Lockdown Mode Limits Tools That Could Enable Data Exfiltration
item.description:
OpenAI has begun rolling out a new Lockdown Mode to ChatGPT for eligible personal accounts to reduce the risk of data exfiltration arising from prompt injection attacks.
The feature is primarily designed for people and organizations that handle sensitive data and require stricter protection guarantees. Lockdown Mode is available to logged-in users across Free, Go, Plus, and Pro, and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Free Apps Are Quietly Turning Smart TVs Into Web-Scraping Proxies for AI
item.description:
A researcher has reverse-engineered the iOS SDK that Bright Data embeds in consumer apps and documented how it turns devices, including always-on smart TVs, into exit nodes that relay web-scraping traffic for a data business Bright Data markets heavily to the AI industry.
The company, the successor to Luminati, operates what it calls the largest residential proxy network in the world,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited SolarWinds Serv-U DoS Flaw to KEV Catalog
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a high-severity security flaw impacting SolarWinds Serv-U multi-protocol file server software to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-28318 (CVSS score: 7.5), is a denial-of-service (DoS) bug that causes the service to crash
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Agent Uncovers 21 Zero-Days in FFmpeg; Chrome Patches Record 429 Bugs
item.description:
Two things landed within days of each other this week. A security startup reported 21 previously unknown vulnerabilities in FFmpeg, the media library inside almost everything that touches video, all of them found by an autonomous AI agent.
The same week, Google shipped Chrome 149 with patches for 429 security bugs, the most ever in a single release.
Only the FFmpeg bugs were found by AI.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Miasma Worm Hits 73 Microsoft GitHub Repositories in Major Supply Chain Attack
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager CVE-2026-20245 Flaw Actively Exploited – No Patch Available
item.description:
Cisco has warned that a high-severity security flaw impacting Catalyst SD-WAN Manager has come under active exploitation.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20245, carries a CVSS score of 7.8 out of a maximum of 10.0. It affects the following deployment types -
On-Prem Deployment Cisco SD-WAN Cloud-Pro Cisco SD-WAN Cloud (Cisco Managed) Cisco SD-WAN for Government (FedRAMP)
"A
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Upgrades, Security Flaws, and SpaceX’s Record IPO Define the Week in Tech
item.description: <p>See what you missed in Daily Tech Insider from June 1–5.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/ai-upgrades-security-flaws-and-spacexs-record-ipo-define-the-week-in-tech/">AI Upgrades, Security Flaws, and SpaceX’s Record IPO Define the Week in Tech</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Cisco Warns Agentic AI Will Put New Pressure on Enterprise Networks
item.description: <p>Cisco executives said agentic AI could triple network traffic, creating new infrastructure and security demands for enterprises.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-cisco-ai-networking-bottleneck/">Cisco Warns Agentic AI Will Put New Pressure on Enterprise Networks</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Drew Robb

item.title: Exposed Fuel Tank Gauges Under Attack in the US
item.description: Threat actors are taking advantage of Internet-exposed tank gauges by breaching gas stations, opening the door to disruption.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: IronWorm and New Miasma Worm Variant Hit npm in Supply Chain Attacks
item.description:
Multiple software supply chain attacks have hit the npm ecosystem, with threat actors using both malicious and poisoned versions of over 50 legitimate packages to distribute a Rust-based information stealer and a self-spreading worm, respectively.
According to JFrog, the information stealer "scrapes every secret it can find on a developer's machine, hides behind an eBPF kernel rootkit, and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Malicious Hugging Face Models Could Trigger Remote Code Execution
item.description: <p>A flaw in Hugging Face Transformers could allow malicious AI models to execute code, exposing credentials and highlighting AI supply chain risks.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-hugging-face-transformers-rce-flaw/">Malicious Hugging Face Models Could Trigger Remote Code Execution</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Chinese Spies Using LinkedIn, Job Sites to Recruit Western Workers
item.description: <p>Five Eyes agencies warn that fake online recruiters linked to Chinese intelligence are targeting workers for sensitive policy and defense information.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-five-eyes-fake-recruiters-chinese-intelligence/">Chinese Spies Using LinkedIn, Job Sites to Recruit Western Workers</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: New CISA Warning: Hackers Are Targeting Fuel Tank Monitoring Systems
item.description: <p>CISA warns attackers are targeting internet-exposed Automatic Tank Gauge systems used in fuel storage. Here’s what operators should fix now.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-cisa-atg-systems-fuel-storage-cyberattacks/">New CISA Warning: Hackers Are Targeting Fuel Tank Monitoring Systems</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Android Spyware Asin Targets Arabic Users via Fake News, PDF and War Map Apps
item.description:
Arabic-speaking users have emerged as the target of a new Android spyware codenamed Asin, according to findings from ESET.
The Slovakian cybersecurity company said it first detected the malware spread via multiple campaigns in early 2025, with each attack wave making use of distinct websites mimicking utilities, war-related updates, and a government news source:
govlens[.]net, which
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: DentaQuest Cyberattack Tied to 2.6M Exposed Accounts
item.description: <p>DentaQuest confirmed a cybersecurity incident after health data tied to 2.6 million accounts surfaced in a public breach listing.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-dentaquest-breach-health-data-2-6m-accounts/">DentaQuest Cyberattack Tied to 2.6M Exposed Accounts</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Adaptive, Agentic AI Worms Loom as Next Enterprise Threat
item.description: AI worms, or &quot;viruses with wings and brains,&quot; adapt to new environments, seek out vulnerabilities, and will likely strike within a year, researchers say.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: AI Worm
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Trump AI Order Seeks Voluntary Frontier Model Testing
item.description: The White House's executive order establishes voluntary framework for early government access to frontier models while investing in federal security.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: New Threat Cluster OP-512 Targets Microsoft IIS Servers with Custom Web Shell Framework
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously unreported threat cluster dubbed OP-512 (where "OP" stands for "opponent") that has been observed&nbsp;targeting Microsoft Internet Information Services (IIS) servers to deploy a bespoke web shell framework.
ReliaQuest has assessed with moderate to high confidence that the espionage-focused activity is linked to China.
"OP-512 was highly
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)
